title: Planning State Transition Closeout
goal: Connect maturity-based planning state to promotion and closeout transitions so ready work becomes active execplans and future-relevant completed work leaves compact residue routing.
non_goals: Do not add heavy workflow automation.
Do not duplicate execplan implementation detail in state.toml.
Do not make archives ordinary active context.

intent.outcome: Ready slices, active execplans, and closed residue records have predictable state transitions.
intent.constraints: Keep the package repo-/agent-/tool-agnostic and keep closed residue compact.
intent.latitude: Use the existing promotion/archive commands and summary projection instead of adding a new command.
intent.escalation: Stop before broad state-machine automation or generated adapter changes.

execution.next_step: Archive this execplan, close #501 and #496, then continue the next shaped lane when selected.
execution.proof: planning package tests, ruff, planning doctor, summary

invariants: Active state points to execplans rather than implementation detail.
Future-relevant residue has a non-archive owner.
Evidence-only completed work can shrink to archive evidence.

intent_continuity.larger intended outcome: Planning state supports progressive refinement and multi-agent handoff through explicit maturity, roles, promotion, and closeout state.

iterative_follow_through.what this slice enabled: Ready work still promotes into active execplans, archive cleanup now removes evidence-only active entries, and future-relevant durable residue is retained as compact closed work_items.
iterative_follow_through.discovered implications: Closed items should not become active context; only future-relevant residue or open-lane closure leaves compact checked-in routing.
iterative_follow_through.proof achieved now: Focused transition tests, full planning package tests, ruff, planning-surface check, doctor, summary, and workspace summary tests passed.

delegated_judgment.requested outcome: Connect maturity-based state with execplan promotion and durable-residue closeout.
delegated_judgment.hard constraints: No duplicate implementation detail in state; no heavy workflow automation.
delegated_judgment.agent may decide locally: Exact compact closed-item fields, summary bucket naming, and focused test coverage.
delegated_judgment.escalate when: The change would require broad state-machine automation or new public command surfaces.

active_milestone.scope: planning state promotion and closeout transitions

validation_commands: cd packages/planning; uv run pytest tests/test_installer.py
cd packages/planning; uv run ruff check .
uv run agentic-workspace doctor --target . --modules planning --format json
completion_criteria: Ready slice promotion still creates an active execplan reference.
Archive cleanup removes evidence-only active state but keeps future-relevant residue as a compact closed work item.
Summary distinguishes routed closed items from closed items that still need residue routing.
#501 is closed with proof and the parent #496 lane is closed or honestly routed.

execution_run.what happened: Added compact closed-work routing for future-relevant archive cleanup, kept evidence-only archive cleanup shrinking state, and exposed routed closed items separately from residue-routing-needed items in summary.
execution_run.scope touched: planning package state/archive logic, tests, active state, and active execplan closeout.
execution_run.validations run: Focused transition tests; full planning package tests; planning package ruff; planning-surface checker; planning doctor; planning summary; workspace summary CLI tests.
execution_run.result for continuation: #501 and the parent #496 lane are satisfied; next broad work should come from another shaped lane.

finished_run_review.scope respected: yes; changes stayed inside planning state transition logic, tests, and planning surfaces.
finished_run_review.intent served: yes; ready-to-active and active-to-closed transitions are covered, and closed residue routing is visible without archive reads.
finished_run_review.config compliance: used compact summary, issue evidence, and checked-in planning state before implementation
finished_run_review.misinterpretation risk: low after tests; closed routed items are compact state records, not active archive context.

proof_report.validation proof: 150 planning package tests passed; planning package ruff passed; planning-surface checker passed; planning doctor and summary returned clean; workspace summary CLI tests passed.
proof_report.evidence for \"proof achieved\" state: Archive transition test proves future-relevant durable residue becomes a closed work_item, while existing active-pointer cleanup behavior still removes evidence-only completed state.

intent_satisfaction.original intent: Connect maturity-based state with execplan promotion and durable-residue closeout.
intent_satisfaction.evidence of intent satisfaction: Promotion from work_items ready slices was already covered and remains passing; archive cleanup now handles active-to-closed residue routing and summary exposes closed routed items separately from missing residue.

system_intent_alignment.relevant system intent: Make planning state compact, explicit, and queryable without duplicate authority.
system_intent_alignment.slice shaping bias: Use commands and projections as the transition boundary, not raw state browsing.
system_intent_alignment.broader-lane validation question: Does this complete the planning-state maturity lane without adding product residue?

closure_check.why this decision is honest: #497 through #501 have landed the planning-state maturity schema, role metadata, summary behavior, state migration, and transition closeout behavior.
closure_check.evidence carried forward: Code and tests now enforce the ready, active, closed, and residue-routing parts of the maturity model.
closure_check.reopen trigger: ready/active/closed transitions regress or closed residue becomes ordinary active context

durable_residue.learned constraint: Future-relevant closeout residue should route into compact checked-in planning state instead of requiring archive reads.
durable_residue.motivation worth preserving: Agents need to distinguish evidence-only completed work from closed work that still owns durable follow-up.
durable_residue.promotion trigger: when archive cleanup or work_maturity projections are touched

execution_summary.outcome delivered: Connected maturity-based planning state to archive closeout residue routing.
execution_summary.validation confirmed: Planning package tests, ruff, planning-surface check, doctor, summary, and workspace summary tests passed.
execution_summary.post-work posterity capture: The transition rules are carried by installer code, tests, and compact closed work_items when future-relevant residue exists.
execution_summary.knowledge promoted (Memory/Docs/Config): No separate Memory or docs promotion needed.

drift_log: 2026-04-28: Promoted #501 from planning-state-maturity lane into an active execplan.
